evaluations.short_rationale: The candidate connects the stated need for difficulty organizing and securing digital credentials and sentimental files for inheritance to a secure, encrypted digital vault with automated release triggers. The prototype—A secure form-based input system that generates a downloadable encrypted PDF manifest—is testable, but the stated claims or data sensitivity require caution.

The candidate connects the stated need for lack of calming mental stimulation during public transit commutes to slow-paced horticultural design where trees grow in real-time synchronization with sunlight exposure in the player's zip code. The prototype—Single desert garden biome where user can plant one cactus and observe sun-path light-tracking simulations over a 48-hour period—is testable, but the stated claims or data sensitivity require caution.

The candidate connects the stated need for high overhead costs for restaurants during off-peak hours to a platform that matches daytime-only cafes with evening-only pop-up chefs. The defined prototype—One cafe hosting three different pop-up chefs over three consecutive weekends—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for lack of structure and social pressure to complete daily tasks to a platform that pairs users for 90-minute silent, video-on co-working sessions. The defined prototype—A simple scheduling calendar that pairs two users for a Zoom session—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for creators struggling to convert free subscribers into paid members to a structured 10-point audit framework specifically for substack pricing page conversion. The defined prototype—Perform 10 manual audits for free in exchange for testimonials and case studies | Define the 10-point audit criteria; Identify 20 potential Substack creators; Conduct 10 —provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for finding open-source creative examples for repetitive academic assignment prompts to searchable registry of verified academic supplemental material. The defined prototype—Directory of three dummy course modules where items can be liked, sorted, and suggested via form submission—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for reconciling billing discrepancies with disparate cloud service vendors to automated cross-referencing of usage logs against invoice line items to identify discrepancies. The defined prototype—A Python script that parses three specific SaaS billing formats and generates a CSV report of flagged discrepancies | Define schema for three target SaaS billing formats;—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for identifying stealth, passive candidate sentiment signals before they actively enter the job market to ranking candidates based on public technical content production. The prototype—A script that takes a GitHub/StackOverflow profile and outputs a score based on frequency and complexity of contributions | Define scoring rubric; Build scraper for one p—offers a concrete initial scope, with implementation details still needing validation.

The candidate connects the stated need for requirement to serve ethical, low-impact high-quality group catering to meals prepared exclusively from 'ugly' or near-expiry restaurant bulk raw materials. The defined prototype—Catering 5 lunches for one specific office office environment focusing on total reduction of carbon-spend data collection—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for losing existing traffic revenue to un-indexed updates in google search result pages to threshold alerts for seo rank fluctuations. The defined prototype—A simple script or manual check that emails users when rank drops below a threshold | Set up rank tracking for 5 sites; Define alert thresholds; Send manual alerts for 30—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for managing community quality during organic spikes of paid users joining chat rooms to threshold filtering allowing new user interactions after passing specific introductory checklist requirements. The defined prototype—Set of manual invite workflow gate protocols installed for three specific private Discord communities—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for identifying stealth, passive candidate sentiment signals before they actively enter the job market to autonomous social reading and contribution analysis, focused solely on long-form public content authored by candidates. The defined prototype—Limited to scraping and grading public contribution histories for one job description on one coding community forum—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for machine downtime disproportionately impacting revenue during slow maintenance call-outs from established vendors to rapid-response modular machine heart swaps and standardized component library to reduce technical failure periods. The defined prototype—Testing speed-to-solution with five broken commercial devices by delivering serviced, reconditioned replacement module parts—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for wasted time on unqualified leads that do not fit the ideal customer profile to an agent that performs deep research on incoming leads by analyzing public digital footprints and company data. The defined prototype—A tool that takes a list of 20 LinkedIn profiles and returns a 'qualified' or 'unqualified' status with a one-sentence justification—provides a bounded first test.

The candidate connects the stated need for inability to find or license high-quality, authentic smartphone unboxing footage to a direct procurement bridge for authentic consumer video assets. The prototype—A closed Slack group vetting 20 creators and 20 brands | Recruit 20 creators; Recruit 20 brands; Facilitate connections in Slack; Monitor quality of output—is testable, but the stated claims or data sensitivity require caution.

The candidate connects the stated need for fresh, raw herbs losing structural viability within days of shop delivery to live, harvestable herbs in the kitchen.
